items.message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

items.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This issue documents a gap in the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` README following PR #58396 (beta.23), which introduced `PlatformHeaders`, `RequestIdMiddleware`, `RequestIdBaggagePropagator`, and consolidated six extension methods into `AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()`. The README needs updating to cover these new APIs and the breaking changes.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` (1.0.0-beta.23)
**Affected API:** `PlatformHeaders` static class, `RequestIdMiddleware`, `RequestIdBaggagePropagator`, `AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()`
**Scenarios:**
- README Key Concepts section is missing a `PlatformHeaders` subsection describing the HTTP header name constants
- `RequestIdMiddleware` behavior and `x-request-id` resolution priority (OTEL trace ID → incoming header → GUID) is not documented
- Breaking change migration from 6 individual extension methods to the consolidated `AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()` pair is not noted in Troubleshooting
- No code example in the Examples section showing `PlatformHeaders` usage

**Root ask:** Update `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` to document the new public APIs and breaking changes, and add a sample test file demonstrating `PlatformHeaders` constants.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation gap — no code defect is involved. The changes are purely additive to the README and sample files.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review PR #58396 to confirm the full scope of public API changes introduced in beta.23
2. Add a `PlatformHeaders` subsection to the **Key Concepts** section of the README with the header name table
3. Add `RequestIdMiddleware` behavior description to Key Concepts or Examples
4. Add a migration note in **Troubleshooting / Logging** for users upgrading from beta.22
5. Add a sample test (`Sample_PlatformHeaders.cs`) and a corresponding README snippet in the **Examples** section
6. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` to wire up the snippet references
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` is a data-plane client library (not `Azure.ResourceManager.*` or `Azure.Provisioning.*`)
- **Service:** `AI Agents` — package lives under `sdk/agentserver/` which maps to the `%AI Agents` ServiceLabel in CODEOWNERS
- **Confidence:** High — package name and service directory unambiguously identify both labels
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %AI Agents` (line 69) — the only ServiceLabel entry for "AI Agents"; matched because the predicted service label is "AI Agents"
- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed
- **ServiceOwners:** `@ankitbko`, `@RaviPidaparthi`
- **Routing action:** Added "Service Attention" label; no assignment (ServiceOwners path, no AzureSdkOwners). Issue is bot-filed (not "customer-reported"), so "needs-team-attention" was not added
- **Scan notes:** Scanned bottom-to-top; no other entry with "AI Agents" was found after line 69; the single entry at line 69–70 is the match
</details>
